After putting their child to bed, the pediatrician spends 20 minutes journaling:  
> “Today, I didn’t write one prescription for a cough syrup. I educated 12 families. I challenged a system that still thinks ‘doing something’ is better than ‘doing nothing.’ But the real work isn’t in my clinic—it’s in changing the culture that equates medicine with care. Tomorrow, I’ll email the medical school dean about adding CCM safety to the pediatric curriculum. We’re not just treating coughs. We’re unlearning a dangerous myth.”
